evals.prompt: Use $organize-important-documents to audit the folder tree in evals/files/audit-case-01. Start with low-risk inspection rather than asking me a bunch of questions. Tell me the best parts, the worst parts, what should be copied into a better system, and what should be moved out of an official-records filing system.
evals.expected_output: A concise audit of the sample folder tree that distinguishes good record-keeping patterns from scope drift, naming problems, and mixed-in working material.
expectations: The response identifies that official records and project material are mixed together.
The response calls out at least one good domain-based grouping worth preserving.
The response recommends moving project or build material out of the records system.
The response does not begin by asking more than five questions.

evals.prompt: Use $organize-important-documents to help my sister build a filing system from scratch. She wants to track identity, taxes, health, housing, and employment records, but she is not sure where they all live yet. Ask only the must-have questions in the first pass, keep it to at most five questions, and then explain what a sensible v1 structure will probably look like.
evals.expected_output: A short first-round interview that asks only blocking questions, keeps the batch small, and starts with what she wants to track rather than where the files currently live.
expectations: The first pass asks no more than five questions.
The questions focus on scope before asking about storage locations.
The response offers examples or defaults to reduce friction.
The response explains that the plan can be incremental.

evals.prompt: Use $organize-important-documents to design an incremental migration plan for someone whose important documents are split across Gmail attachments, Google Drive, Downloads, and a paper file box. She wants Google Drive as the primary home, needs a lightweight upkeep routine, and wants to know what she should do manually versus what she can ask an LLM to help with regularly.
evals.expected_output: A concrete migration and maintenance plan with a proposed folder structure, naming conventions, source-of-truth guidance, and a split between manual tasks and LLM-assisted tasks.
expectations: The response proposes a domain-based root layout with inbox and archive areas.
The response distinguishes manual tasks from LLM-assisted tasks.
The response recommends a single source of truth and a backup model.
The response calls out what should stay outside the official records system.
